explanations.9: This uses a CSS variable (--ifm-color-emphasis-200) that's defined elsewhere in your project, so the actual color depends on your theme setup.
explanations.29: This uses flexbox (display: flex) to arrange child elements horizontally; align-items: center vertically aligns them to the middle.
explanations.35: This creates a vertical stack of elements (flex-direction: column means items go down instead of across).
explanations.42: The border-radius: 50% trick makes a square element into a perfect circle by rounding it completely.
explanations.56: The display: block makes this element take up full width and start on a new line, even though it's probably a span or inline element.
explanations.60: margin-left: auto pushes this element to the right side by eating up all available space to its left.
explanations.73: The \a0 is a non-breaking space character that creates spacing after the heart emoji.
explanations.99: display: inline-flex lets this link use flexbox layout while staying in the text flow instead of taking up a full line.
explanations.106: This selector targets img elements that have the blueskyCommentEmbedThumb class (the type matters here).
explanations.112: flex-wrap: wrap means if images run out of space horizontally, they'll move to the next line instead of squishing.
explanations.159: This transition makes the background color and text color fade smoothly over 0.2 seconds when the button state changes.
explanations.169: This @media query applies different styles when the user's system is set to dark mode.
